pattern: Union-find merges every pair joined by equality, then a single pass over the inequalities reports a contradiction whenever two supposedly-unequal variables share a root.
hints: Equalities force variables into the same group; inequalities forbid two variables from sharing a group.
Process all the equals first so the groups are fully formed before you check the not-equals.
Use a disjoint-set over the 26 letters: union the two sides of every '==' equation, then for every '!=' equation return false if find(x) == find(y); otherwise return true.

edge_inputs.description: transitive equality breaks a later inequality
long equality chain then a contradiction

stress.description: random equations over a few letters so chains and conflicts appear
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 500))
    letters = 'abcdef'
    eqs = []
    for _ in range(n):
        x = rng.choice(letters)
        y = rng.choice(letters)
        op = '==' if rng.random() < 0.6 else '!='
        eqs.append(x + op + y)
    return [eqs]
stress.note: small alphabet maximizes the chance of both consistent merges and conflicts
